exec_args.query: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table';

args.query:
SELECT "Index",
SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days,
SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days
FROM index_trade
WHERE "Date" BETWEEN '2018-01-01' AND '2018-12-31'
  AND "Index" IN ('GSPTSE','IXIC','NYA')
GROUP BY "Index";

exec_args.sql:
SELECT "Index",
SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days,
SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days
FROM index_trade
WHERE "Date" BETWEEN '2018-01-01' AND '2018-12-31'
  AND "Index" IN ('GSPTSE','IXIC','NYA')
GROUP BY "Index";

result.preview: "GSPTSE (44 up days, 41 down days) and IXIC (44 up days, 31 down days) had more up days than down days in 2018. NYA had more down days (36 up days, 42 down days)."

args.answer: GSPTSE (44 up days, 41 down days) and IXIC (44 up days, 31 down days) had more up days than down days in 2018. NYA had more down days (36 up days, 42 down days).

val_args.answer: GSPTSE (44 up days, 41 down days) and IXIC (44 up days, 31 down days) had more up days than down days in 2018. NYA had more down days (36 up days, 42 down days).
